texto: You have made some notes, read some writing books, and done some research. Mostly what you've done is talk about writing a book. An idea for a book is not a book; it is a waste of time. There is no singular thing that makes someone a writer, but there is one thing that makes someone a joke--talking about writing a book without doing any work.
explicacao: Citação de Pat Walsh sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “You have made some notes, read some writing books, and done some research. Mostly what you've don…” articula trabalho, publishing, writing em registro de trabalho, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e writing.

frase_original: You have made some notes, read some writing books, and done some research. Mostly what you've done is talk about writing a book. An idea for a book is not a book; it is a waste of time. There is no singular thing that makes someone a writer, but there is one thing that makes someone a joke--talking about writing a book without doing any work.

texto: We do not like the truth because it is simple, we do not want the truth because it is hard, and we do not trust the truth because it is free. Perhaps because many are idealists and publishing is so frustrating, writers are particularly vulnerable to believing in those who offer hope in exchange for cash. Writers know life is tough and we all want to think of an easier way. Maybe for a rare few, there is. If you count on that, you are a chump and somebody is going to take your money and break your heart.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Pat Walsh: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “We do not like the truth because it is simple, we do not want the truth because it is hard, and w…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, publishing, writing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e writing.
